Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Results section explicitly states OsPAL1 as LOC_Os02g41630 in section 2.3.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The paper inconsistently calls OsPAL1 a 'phenylalanine aminotransferase' in Results, while context and cited literature imply PAL. Exact full name 'Phenylalanine ammonia-lyase 1' is not explicitly stated in the paper text provided.

EC_Number.reason: EC 4.3.1.24 does not appear in the paper.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: Abstract and Results explicitly state OsPAL1 is involved in the phenylpropanoids/phenylpropanoid biosynthesis pathway.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Results section 2.6 links PAL activity to lignin and flavonoid contents, supporting OsPAL1 as an entry step supplying these branches.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results show manipulation of the PibH8-OsPAL module affects lignin and flavonoids content.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Lignin and flavonoids are explicitly named; their classes as phenylpropanoid polymer and flavonoid are standard and directly consistent with the named metabolites.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Results and Discussion associate increased lignin/flavonoids with improved drought resistance, and the paper also links this module to blast resistance.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The paper measures lignin/flavonoids in transgenic lines and mentions drought-stressed leaf samples elsewhere, but does not explicitly specify 'seedling tissues/leaves' as the accumulation site for these metabolites.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Rice corresponds to Oryza sativa, the study species.

Applied_Species.reason: Functional tests were performed in rice.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Applied species is rice, Oryza sativa.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: OsPAL1 knockout and overexpression lines were generated in the ZH11 background.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Section 2.3 says expression at seedling stage was examined and OsPAL5 was highest except OsPAL1, implying OsPAL1 is among the highest expressed at seedling stage.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Results state PAL family has nine members in rice; OsPAL1 is one of them.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Physiological measurements section uses L-phenylalanine as substrate for PAL activity, consistent with OsPAL1 function in the paper context.

Primary_Product.reason: trans-cinnamic acid and ammonia are not explicitly stated in the paper text provided.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The reaction is biologically consistent with PAL, but the paper does not explicitly describe OsPAL1 converting L-phenylalanine to trans-cinnamic acid in the provided text.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: Results link PAL activity to lignin and flavonoid accumulation, supporting precursor supply for these end products.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Abstract and Discussion describe OsPAL1/PAL as a rate-limiting enzyme in phenylpropanoid pathway, supporting an upstream entry/committed step role.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Abstract explicitly calls OsPAL1 'a rate-limiting enzyme in phenylpropanoids biosynthesis pathway in rice.'

Interacting_Proteins.reason: PibH8 and OsFBK16 interactions with OsPAL1 are supported; direct interaction with OsPAL5 is not shown, so listing OsPAL5 as an interacting protein for OsPAL1 is unsupported.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: OsPAL1 knockout reduced drought resistance and overexpression increased it; the module is connected to lignin/flavonoid accumulation.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each point is supported: physical interaction with PibH8, drought phenotypes of mutants/OE, epistasis showing PibH8 acts upstream of OsPAL1, and linkage to lignin/flavonoids through the pathway module.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods appear in Results or associated figures/supporting figures, including CRISPR/OE, Y2H, BiFC, SFLC, Co-IP, pull-down, PAL activity, and epistasis with PibH8-OE/ospal1.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: OsPAL1 drought phenotype was directly tested at seedling stage; panicle development stage testing is shown for PibH8, not explicitly for OsPAL1.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper indeed inconsistently uses 'phenylalanine aminotransferase' for OsPAL1 despite PAL activity/pathway context indicating PAL function.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a plausible interpretation, but the paper does not directly discuss breeding value of OsPAL1 or compare stacking strategies with PibH8/OsFBK16.

Omics_Data.reason: RNA-seq of drought-stressed leaf samples was used to prioritize genes in the GWAS interval, but not specifically reported as omics data for OsPAL1; 'accession NA' is not a paper statement.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Section 2.3 states OsPAL5 is most closely related to OsPAL1 and has obviously higher expression than other PAL members except OsPAL1 at seedling stage.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Likely an entry PAL step, but not explicitly established for OsPAL5 in this paper.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The field carefully says not directly established in this paper; the paper only places OsPAL5 as a PAL family entry enzyme contextually.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Section 2.4 says the OsPAL5 mutant line generated by CRISPR-Cas9 also showed reduced drought resistance.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All listed points are supported by Results and supporting figures: close homolog/high expression, interaction with PibH8, mutant drought phenotype, and protection via competition with OsFBK16.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Each listed method is described in Results/supporting figures for OsPAL5-related analyses.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper indeed treats OsPAL5 as a secondary, not primary, mechanistic focus.

Regulator_Type.reason: PibH8 is identified as an NLR and shown to regulate PAL protein stability through competitive interaction with OsFBK16.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Figure 3a and text state PibH8 contains CC, NB-ARC, and LRR domains; Discussion calls it a typical CNL-type NLR.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results show PibH8 affects lignin and flavonoid content.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Lignin and flavonoids are explicitly the affected metabolites.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper links increased lignin/flavonoids to improved drought resistance and also associates the pathway with blast resistance.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: The paper uses natural accessions, ZH11, HHZ, KY131, and W119 in different analyses.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: RNA-seq showed strong expression change after drought; DR haplotype had higher drought-induced expression than DS; NIL comparisons under drought but not normal conditions are explicitly described.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: Drought stress and promoter SNP vg0826989447 (C>T) defining haplotypes are explicitly described.

Regulation_Mode.reason: Section 2.6 explicitly shows competitive binding to OsFBK16 to protect PAL proteins from degradation.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: OsPAL1 is directly established and OsPAL5 is additionally supported in supplementary interaction/competition assays.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: The paper shows PibH8 stabilizes OsPAL1 and suggests similar protection of OsPAL5 by competing with OsFBK16, increasing PAL activity and downstream metabolites.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: Results explicitly connect PibH8 to phenylpropanoid metabolism via PAL stability.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Interactions with OsPAL1, OsPAL5, and OsFBK16 are shown in Results/supporting figures.

Protein_Complex_Involvement.reason: The paper demonstrates a competitive interaction module among PibH8, OsPAL1/5, and OsFBK16.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: PibH8 positively regulates lignin/flavonoid accumulation, drought resistance, and blast resistance.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: This accurately summarizes the supported mechanism that PibH8 protects PALs, enabling accumulation of drought-protective phenylpropanoids.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All six points are explicitly supported by GWAS, transgenics, interaction assays, competition/stability data, epistasis, and haplotype/NIL analyses.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are reported in the paper or supporting figures.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Drought at seedling and panicle stages, field drought, and blast fungus infection were all tested.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Native promoter haplotypes and 35S promoter in transient assays are supported, but 'overexpression constructs in pCAMBIA1301U promoter details not fully stated' is partly editorial inference rather than a direct paper statement.

Other_Important_Info.reason: PibH8 is described as a homolog of Pib, and superior Hap.1 improves both resistances without obvious agronomic penalties in NILs under normal conditions.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper explicitly emphasizes PibH8-Hap.1 as a promising breeding target/value for improving drought and blast resistance, especially in KY131.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper states no significant differences in major agronomic traits under normal conditions for the NILs.

Omics_Data.reason: GWAS with 7,367,740 variants and RNA-seq of drought-stressed leaf samples are explicitly described; 'accession NA' is acceptable as no accession is given in the paper.

Regulator_Type.reason: OsFBK16 is explicitly described as an E3 ubiquitin ligase.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The name implies F-box/Kelch, but the paper text provided does not explicitly spell out 'F-box/Kelch repeat-containing protein'.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Through regulation of PAL/PibH8, OsFBK16 is connected in Results to lignin and flavonoid accumulation.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Lignin and flavonoids are the terminal metabolites affected in the described pathway module.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper presents lignin/flavonoids as protective secondary metabolites contributing to stress resistance.

Source_Species.reason: OsFBK16 is a rice gene in this study.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: OsFBK16 knockout and OE lines were generated in ZH11 background.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: Semi-in vitro ubiquitination assay showed OsFBK16 ubiquitination activity toward PibH8 was enhanced under drought conditions.

Regulation_Mode.reason: Results show OsFBK16 promotes ubiquitin-mediated degradation of PibH8 and is known to degrade PALs.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: OsPAL1 and OsPAL5 are cited as known targets, and this study adds PibH8 as a target.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: Supported by prior report cited in Results for OsPAL1/5 and by this study for PibH8; PAL activity decreases accordingly.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: The paper supports negative post-translational control of phenylpropanoid biosynthesis via PAL stability.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Interactions with PibH8 are directly shown; OsPAL1/OsPAL5 interactions are referenced from prior work and incorporated into the model tested here.

Protein_Complex_Involvement.reason: The competitive interaction network with PibH8 and PAL proteins is explicitly investigated.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Knockout increased drought resistance and overexpression reduced it; PAL activity was reduced in OE lines.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: This is a fair summary of the supported mechanism involving OsPAL1 and PibH8 stability.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All four points are directly supported by Results and figures/supporting figures.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods were used for OsFBK16-related conclusions.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Drought stress was the tested condition.

Applied_Promoters.reason: 35S promoter use in transient assays is supported, but 'overexpression construct details otherwise NA' is not a paper statement.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly says previous work showed OsFBK16 negatively regulates blast resistance through degrading PALs, and this study extends its function to drought resistance and PibH8 targeting.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Negative-target editing is a reasonable interpretation, but explicit breeding application of OsFBK16 is not directly discussed in the paper.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Possible broad off-target effects of E3 ligase perturbation are speculative and were not tested in this study.

corrections.old_value: Possible off-target effects due to broad ubiquitin-ligase functions are plausible but not tested in this study.
